qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OAuth2 authentication bypass via OAuth2Required defaulting to false
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory root cause is a missing return after sending HTTP 404 in HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdPut (api_datarepository.go), allowing path validation bypass for influence data PUT requests. This finding describes a different root cause: OAuth2Required bool defaulting to false in context.go:72, causing AuthorizationCheck to return nil for all requests. Different vulnerable function, different missing guard, different control flow.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via user-controlled subscription callback URLs
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory root cause is a missing return after HTTP 404 in the influence data PUT handler (api_datarepository.go), allowing creation/modification of subscriptions with invalid influenceId. This finding describes SSRF via unvalidated callbackReference/notificationUri URLs stored in subscriptions and used for outbound HTTP POST notifications (callback.go). Different vulnerable function, different missing validation, different vulnerability class.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes a missing return statement after sending HTTP 404 in HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdPut (api_datarepository.go), which allows unauthenticated creation/modification of Traffic Influence Subscriptions via PUT requests with arbitrary influenceId values. Neither qualifying finding identifies this root cause. Finding vuln-oauth2-default-bypass describes an OAuth2 authentication bypass due to OAuth2Required defaulting to false (context.go:72), a different missing guard in a different function. Finding vuln-ssrf-subscription-callback describes SSRF via unvalidated callback URLs in notification functions (callback.go), a different vulnerability class and different missing validation. Both findings are in the same UDR codebase but address different root causes than the advisory-described vulnerability.
evidence: Advisory root cause: missing return after c.String(http.StatusNotFound, ...) in HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdPut in api_datarepository.go
vuln-oauth2-default-bypass root cause: OAuth2Required bool defaults to false in context.go:72, AuthorizationCheck returns nil in context.go:237-240
vuln-ssrf-subscription-callback root cause: no URL validation on callbackReference/notificationUri in callback.go and subs_to_notify_collection.go
Neither finding references the HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdPut function or the missing return after 404 response
